title: Module 1
content:
Metals - Definition of Biomaterials – Classification – Metals-Stainless
steel-Co-Cr alloys-Ti based alloys- Dental Metals -Other metals –corrosion
of metallic implants-manufacturing implants-mechanical properties-
applications in medicine. Biocompatibility-host and tissue response- in-
vitro & in-vivo tests for implants. Ceramics - Ceramic materials-
Aluminium oxides, -Glass ceramics, carbons. Bioactivity -Bioactive glass
& its applications. Non absorbable or bio inert ceramics-degradable or
resorbable ceramics-bioactive ceramics-deterioration of ceramics-Bioactive
ceramic manufacturing techniques- applications in medicine. Strengthening
ceramics for biomedical applications.

title: Module 2
content:
Polymers - Polymerization and Basic Structure- Polymers Used as
Biomaterials-
Polyvinylchloride
(PVC)
-Polyethylene
(PE)
-
Polypropylene-(PP) –Poly methyl methacrylate (PMMA). Biodegradable
Polymers-surface Modifications for Improving Biocompatibility- Chemo
gradient Surfaces for Cell and Protein Interaction- Synthetic degradable and
non-bio degradable polymers.
Natural polymers and their derivatives for medical use- Polyelectrolyte
Complexes (PECs)- Overview - Biomedical Applications-Formation of
PECs-Types of PECs- Applications of PECs-Wound healing, drug delivery.

title: Module 3
content:
Composites- Natural composites-bone, cartilage - Structure – properties-
anisotropy - particulate composites - fibrous composites - porous materials
-   biocompatibility of composites. Design and fabrication methods for
biocomposites-
Production
techniques
for
biocomposite
parts-
Conventionalcomposite processing techniques- Extrusion and injection for
thermoplastic materials- Filament winding - compression -  infusion -
autoclaving - Hard tissue applications of biocomposites-soft tissue
applications of biocomposites.

title: Module 4
content:
3-D printing systems for Tissue Engineering - Basics of 3D printing
technology- Bioinks for 3D printing of artificial extracellular matrices-
Printing technologies used in three dimensional printing-Application of 3D
printing- Scaffold based bioink materials-hydrogels- Nanotechnology based
bioink-Artificial extracellular matrix-3D-printed polymeric scaffolds for
bone tissue engineering- 3D-printed natural and synthetic polymeric
scaffolds for bone tissue engineering- Synthetic 3D-bioprinted scaffolds-
polymer based composite scaffolds-Cellulosic materials as bioinks for 3D
printing applications-Cellulose for biomedical applications- wound healing-
Bioresponsive implants-drug delivery.
